data.title: Iran Claims U.S. F-16 Hit in New Military Action
data.description:
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has announced that a U.S. F-16 fighter jet was struck during a recent wave of military actions. According to RTHK, the Revolutionary Guard stated that missiles and drones targeted several American and Israeli heavy industrial enterprises in Israel and other locations, causing damage to some facilities. The statement warned that any attacks on Iranian industrial facilities by the U.S. would be met with retaliation.

Additionally, Iran's Fars News Agency reported that the Iranian Intelligence Ministry recently conducted operations across multiple provinces, resulting in the elimination of five separatist terrorists and the capture of 19 American and Israeli mercenaries.

In a separate development, Iranian President Ebrahim Raisi, during a conversation with Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, expressed concerns over Iran being subjected to military aggression twice despite engaging in dialogue and diplomatic efforts. He highlighted that although U.S. officials have promised not to target Iran's economic and energy infrastructure, these facilities have become targets, leading to increased distrust of the U.S. due to its contradictory actions and statements.

data.title: Federal Reserve Bans Regions Bank Employee for Theft
data.description: The Federal Reserve Board has permanently banned a Regions Bank employee from working in any banking institution following the theft of $327,500 from a customer. According to NS3.AI, the employee illicitly withdrew $323,500 from the customer's accounts over a period from April 2022 to July 2024 and additionally took $4,000 from the customer's cash box.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Last Co-Founder Departs Elon Musk's xAI Amid Restructuring
data.description: According to Jin10, Business Insider reports that the last remaining member of the initial co-founding team at Elon Musk's xAI, Ross Nordeen, has left the company. Nordeen, one of the 11 original co-founders alongside Musk, departed this week. His employee identification on the X platform, which previously indicated his affiliation with xAI, has also been removed. Nordeen, aged 36, worked directly under Musk, serving as a key assistant responsible for coordinating internal priorities and driving task execution within the company. His departure comes as Musk is restructuring xAI and preparing for a major initial public offering of his rocket company, SpaceX.

data.title: Data Breach at Navia Benefit Solutions Exposes Personal Information of Over 2.6 Million Americans
data.description: Navia Benefit Solutions has reported a security breach that compromised the personal data of 2,697,540 individuals in the United States. According to NS3.AI, the breach occurred between December 22nd and January 15th, during which an unauthorized party accessed sensitive information. The exposed data includes names, dates of birth, Social Security numbers, and contact details. The company is currently addressing the situation and taking steps to enhance its security measures to prevent future incidents.

data.title: Wintermute Introduces 24/7 Crude Oil CFD Trading with Crypto Collateral
data.description: Wintermute has unveiled a new crude oil CFD offering that operates around the clock, allowing clients to use fiat or cryptocurrency as collateral and engage in trading through OTC channels. According to NS3.AI, this development reflects a growing demand for oil exposure outside traditional trading hours. In March, an oil-linked perpetual on Hyperliquid achieved over $1.2 billion in 24-hour trading volume, highlighting the increasing interest in extended trading hours and tokenized market access within the financial sector.

data.title: U.S. Amphibious Assault Ship Arrives in Central Command Area
data.description: On March 29, U.S. military personnel, including Marines, have arrived in the Central Command area aboard the amphibious assault ship 'Tripoli.' According to BlockBeats, this deployment marks a strategic positioning within the region. The U.S. Central Command oversees operations in the Middle East and parts of Central Asia, and the arrival of the 'Tripoli' is part of ongoing military activities in these areas.

data.title: Trump Media & Technology Group Ventures into Power Generation with Unconventional Technology
data.description: Trump Media & Technology Group is making a significant move into the power generation sector with a $6 billion investment. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that the company is partnering with an unexpected collaborator to explore a technology that has not yet yielded any electricity output. This venture marks a notable shift for the group, traditionally associated with media and technology, as it seeks to diversify its portfolio and explore new industries. The specifics of the technology and the identity of the partner remain undisclosed, raising questions about the feasibility and potential impact of this ambitious project. The initiative reflects a broader trend of media companies branching out into diverse sectors, although the success of such ventures often hinges on the viability of the technology and the strength of the partnerships involved.

data.title: Brazil Enacts Law to Confiscate and Sell Criminal Assets, Including Cryptocurrency
data.description: Brazil has enacted a new law aimed at combating organized crime by allowing authorities to confiscate and sell assets, including cryptocurrency. According to NS3.AI, the legislation permits informants to receive up to 5% of the recovered assets. The proceeds from these sales are designated for funding police equipment, training, and special operations. Additionally, assets linked to drug trafficking will be allocated to the federal anti-drug policy fund. The law also increases penalties for violent criminal organizations, paramilitary groups, and private militias.

data.title: Brazil has signed an anti-gang law that allows confiscated cryptocurrency to be used to fund law enforcement.
data.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva signed an anti-gang bill on Tuesday, granting authorities new powers to freeze and confiscate assets during investigations and after convictions. These assets include cryptocurrencies, stocks, and luxury goods. The bill allows authorities to permanently seize and sell these assets, with proceeds used for police equipment upgrades, training, and special operations. It also establishes economic incentives for public assistance in law enforcement, offering up to 5% of the seized assets as a reward to informants who provide information and help recover assets. Assets seized related to drug trafficking will be included in the federal anti-drug policy fund, not the security fund. Furthermore, the bill increases penalties for extreme violent criminal organizations, paramilitary groups, and private militias that use violence or serious threats to control territory, disrupt public services, attack infrastructure, or intimidate authorities and civilians.

data.title: GMX DAO Conducts Significant GMX Token Buyback
data.description: GMX DAO has announced a substantial buyback of GMX tokens. According to PANews, the organization revealed on the X platform that between March 18 and March 24, it repurchased 18,500 GMX tokens for approximately $120,000, with an average price of around $6.49 per token. Since the initiation of the buyback program on March 5, GMX DAO has acquired a total of 48,280 GMX tokens, investing roughly $320,000 at an average price of about $6.63 per token.

data.title: Ark Invest continued to reduce its holdings in tech stocks such as Nvidia on Friday.
data.description: According to Ark Invest Tracker, following its reduction in tech stocks and crypto ETFs on Thursday, Ark Invest continued to reduce its holdings in tech stocks such as NVIDIA on Friday. This included selling more than 58,000 shares of NVIDIA and more than 19,000 shares of AMD, representing 0.1%-0.15% of the fund's total value. At the same time, it bought 48,600 shares of biotechnology company Arcturus Therapeutics.

data.title: Ark Invest Reduces Holdings in Tech Stocks, Shifts Focus to Biotech
data.description: Ark Invest has significantly reduced its holdings in technology stocks, including NVIDIA and AMD, following a similar move the previous day. According to BlockBeats, the investment firm sold over 58,000 shares of NVIDIA and more than 19,000 shares of AMD, which represent 0.1% to 0.15% of the fund's total value. In contrast, Ark Invest purchased 48,600 shares of biotech company Arcturus Therapeutics, indicating a strategic shift from AI chips to medical innovation.

data.title: World Foundation Completes $65 Million OTC Sale with Four Counterparties
data.description: World Foundation announced that its subsidiary, World Assets, Ltd., has completed over-the-counter sales totaling $65 million with four counterparties over the past week. According to Foresight News, the first transaction is set to settle on March 20, 2026. The average sale price was approximately $0.2719 per token, with $25 million worth of tokens subject to a six-month lock-up period. The proceeds from these sales will be allocated to core operations, research and development, Orb manufacturing, and ecosystem development. Settlement transactions will be conducted through World Assets' multi-signature address starting with 0xE797.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Google to Back Texas Data Center Project for Anthropic
data.description: Google is set to support a Texas data center project leased to Anthropic, with the initial phase potentially surpassing $5 billion, according to a report. According to NS3.AI, this development comes as a U.S. federal judge has temporarily blocked the Pentagon from designating Anthropic as a national security risk, thereby preventing the halt of government use of its AI tools.

data.title: AI Agents Poised to Capture Arbitrage in Prediction Markets
data.description: Industry executives have indicated that AI agents are likely to play a growing role in capturing arbitrage opportunities within prediction markets. According to NS3.AI, a study has estimated that approximately $40 million has been extracted from pricing inefficiencies in these markets. Executives also noted that while faster automation could enhance efficiency, it may also elevate the risks of market manipulation, particularly in thinly traded markets. This potential for increased manipulation underscores the need for implementing robust guardrails to ensure market integrity.

data.title: Google plans to support Anthropic's $5 billion data center project.
data.description: Google is reportedly preparing to back a large data center project in Texas, operated by Nexus Data Centers and leased to Anthropic. According to the Financial Times, citing sources familiar with the matter, the initial phase of the project could exceed $5 billion, with Google expected to provide construction loans, while a consortium of banks is also vying to secure financing by mid-year. Anthropic recently signed a lease for the 2,800-acre campus, part of its broader infrastructure partnership with Google. Construction has begun, with early debt financing provided by publicly traded closed-end investment firm Eagle Point. The site is expected to provide approximately 500 megawatts of capacity by the end of 2026, with the potential to expand to 7.7 gigawatts in the future. Meanwhile, U.S. District Judge Rita Lin in San Francisco issued a preliminary injunction on Thursday, temporarily blocking the Pentagon's directive to designate Anthropic as a national security risk and halt government use of its AI tools. The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit filed by Anthropic. The judge called the government's actions "arbitrary" and warned against designating a U.S. company as a threat without clear legal grounds. Anthropic had reportedly strained relations with the government after refusing to allow its models to be used in lethal autonomous weapons or mass surveillance.

data.title: Michael Saylor posted that "it's time to put on laser eyes again," seemingly signaling a bullish outlook.
data.description: Michael Saylor posted on social media, "It's time to put the laser eyes back on," seemingly signaling a bullish outlook.

data.title: Expert: Bitcoin's Monthly RSI Indicates Potential for Significant Gains
data.description: Bitgrow Lab founder Vivek Sen has highlighted that Bitcoin's monthly Relative Strength Index (RSI) has entered an extreme oversold zone, a rare occurrence that has happened only four times in the cryptocurrency's history. According to NS3.AI, Sen noted that previous instances of this RSI condition were followed by substantial gains, with increases reaching up to 700%.

data.title: Michael Saylor Signals Optimism for Bitcoin with 'Laser Eyes' Comment
data.description: Michael Saylor, founder and executive chairman of Bitcoin treasury firm Strategy, recently expressed a bullish sentiment on social media. According to PANews, Saylor stated, "It's time to put the laser eyes back on," signaling optimism for Bitcoin's future. The "laser eyes" symbol was previously used by the crypto community as a sign of support during bull markets, suggesting Saylor's positive outlook for Bitcoin's market prospects.

data.title: U.S. Navy Aircraft Carrier Ford Arrives in Croatia's Split Port
data.description: On March 28, the Croatian Ministry of Defense announced via its official social media account that the U.S. Navy's nuclear-powered aircraft carrier, Ford, entered the waters of Split Port in Croatia. According to BlockBeats, the Croatian government had previously approved the carrier's entry into Croatian internal waters and its docking at Split Port. The arrival of the Ford is part of its scheduled visit to Split Port for maintenance activities.
